sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 74, level 2.
Valid actions right now: LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'LEFT': 'Moves player left ~6 columns; can trigger board reconfiguration', 'MOUSE': 'Clicks on position - collects green blocks VERIFIED(step 2)', 'RIGHT': 'Moves player right ~6 columns; can trigger board reconfiguration VERIFIED(step 14)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'background': 'black with gray dots', 'green_blocks': 'light green 21-pixel blocks, collectable by MOUSE', 'pink_blocks': 'pink cross shape, not collectable by MOUSE', 'player': 'blue+yellow small object', 'platforms': 'large sky blue regions', 'purple_blocks_top': 'purple blocks with yellow/white dots at top of level 2'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'purple (left) + white (right) at row 63 on level 1; white at row 63 on level 2'}
- Win pattern: {'level1': 'Collect green blocks with MOUSE, move LEFT toward pink cross to complete level'}
- Level log: {'level1': 'Completed in 50 actions - collect greens with MOUSE, approach pink cross, LEFT to complete', 'level2_attempt1': 'Game over after 9 MOUSE clicks on green blocks - blocks NOT collected, approach failed'}
- Cross-level notes: Level 1: collect greens, approach pink, LEFT. Level 2 has different mechanics - MOUSE on green blocks didn't work.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 23 of about 48 target actions so far.

[LEDGER COMPACTION]
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'LEFT': 'Moves player left ~6 columns', 'MOUSE': 'Clicks on position - collects green blocks VERIFIED(step 2) on level 1; on level 2 clicking green blocks collects them but game over triggers after ~8-9 clicks', 'RIGHT': 'Moves player right ~6 columns'}
- Object taxonomy: {'background': 'black with gray dots', 'green_blocks': 'light green 21-pixel blocks in 2x7 grid, collectable by MOUSE', 'purple_blocks_top': '3 purple blocks at rows 7-10, cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28 with yellow/white dot patterns below at row 11', 'player': 'blue+yellow small object at bottom of level', 'platforms': 'large sky blue regions'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'purple+white at row 63, tracks progress with purple pixels'}
- Win pattern: {'level1': 'Collect green blocks with MOUSE, move LEFT toward pink cross to complete level'}
- Level log: {'level1': 'Completed in 50 actions - collect greens with MOUSE, approach pink cross, LEFT to complete', 'level2': 'In progress - game over triggers after ~8-9 MOUSE clicks, resets level'}
- Cross-level notes: Level 2: game over triggers after ~8-9 clicks and resets level. Need different approach than batch collecting.
Current level:
- World model: Level 2 - 14 green blocks (2x7 grid), 3 purple blocks at top with dot patterns. Bottom bar tracks progress with purple pixels. Just moved LEFT.
- Goal model: Collect all green blocks without triggering game over. May need to move player between collections or interact with purple blocks first.
- Recent findings: ['Game over after 8-9 MOUSE clicks resets level VERIFIED(step 73)', 'Clicking white dot at row 11 col 21 added 1 purple pixel to bottom bar', 'Clicking purple block at row 8 col 15 added 1 purple pixel', 'Clicking green block at row 27 col 15 collected it (added 1 purple pixel)', 'Moving LEFT did not cause game over']
- Open questions: ['Does moving player enable collecting more green blocks?', 'Is there a specific order to collect blocks?', 'Do purple blocks at top need to be clicked first?']
- Plan: After moving LEFT, try collecting green blocks one at a time with LEFT/RIGHT moves between them to avoid game over
- Strategy log: ['Batch MOUSE on green blocks: game over after 8 clicks (step 72)', 'Direct collecting without player movement causes game over']
- Failed probes: ['Batch MOUSE on all 13 green blocks: game over after 8 clicks (step 72)', '9 MOUSE clicks on green blocks: game over without collection (step 59)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
LEFT moved the player but the board looks the same. Let me check what changed - the player position should have changed. Now I need to try a different approach. Let me check the bottom bar to see if purple pixels changed, and then try collecting green blocks one at a time with player movement between them.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The game over triggers after ~8-9 clicks. Maybe the solution is to collect green blocks one at a time, but with player movement (LEFT/RIGHT) between each click to "reset" some counter. Let me try: click one green block, move LEFT, click another, move LEFT, etc.
But wait - I need to be efficient. Let me first check if the bottom bar changed after LEFT, and try collecting just one green block to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Check bottom bar purple pixels
purple = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'p']
bottom_purple = [p for p in purple if any(x[0] == 63 for x in p['boundary'])]
total_purple = sum(p['pixels'] for p in bottom_purple)
print(f"Bottom purple pixels: {total_purple}")

# Find player position
player = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b']
if player:
    b = player[0]['boundary']
    print(f"Player at: min_row={min(x[0] for x in b)}, min_col={min(x[1] for x in b)}")

# Try: collect one green block, then move, to see if interleaving avoids game over
# First collect one green block
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 15}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"LEFT": "Moves player left ~6 columns", "MOUSE": "Clicks on position - collects green blocks but game over after ~8 clicks", "RIGHT": "Moves player right ~6 columns"}, "object_taxonomy": {"background": "black with gray dots", "green_blocks": "light green 21-pixel blocks", "purple_blocks_top": "purple blocks with dots at top", "player": "blue+yellow small object", "platforms": "large sky blue regions"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "purple+white at row 63"}, "win_pattern": {"level1": "Collect greens, move LEFT to pink cross"}, "level_log": {"level1": "Completed", "level2": "In progress, game over after 8 clicks"}, "cross_level_notes": "Level 2 resets after game over"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 2 reset state, 14 green blocks, just moved LEFT", "goal_model": "Collect green blocks without game over", "recent_findings": ["LEFT moved player without game over"], "open_questions": ["Does interleaving movement with clicks avoid game over?"], "current_plan": "Test collecting one green block after LEFT movement", "strategy_log": ["Batch collecting fails"], "failed_probes": ["Batch MOUSE: game over after 8 clicks"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Bottom purple pixels: 1
Player at: min_row=37, min_col=15
